title: Ahoi Chalisa
intro: Shri Ahoi Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Goddess Ahoi
description:
|| Shri Ahoi Mata Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Ahoi Mata vinati suno
Sevak ki rakho laaj
Santan sukh samriddhi do
Puran ho sab kaaj

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Ahoi Ambe Jagadamba
Sada sahay karo sukh kamba
Nari tav vrat karti pyari
Sut sukh paaye sada sukhkari
Nirdhan dhani heen sukh paave
Daridra mite vaibhav barsaave
Sinhasin tu jagat Bhavani
Bhakt bachavan wali rani
Dhoop deep naivedya chadhava
Bhakt manovanchhit phal paava
Putravati nari sukh paave
Putraheen ke putra basaave
Sukh sampatti dehi Jagadamba
Dukh daridra mite sab kamba
Ashtami tithi vrat jo nari
Karahi shraddha sahit taiyari
Sada suhagin vah nari hoi
Kripa karen Jagjanani Ahoi

|| Doha ||
Ahoi Mata ki kripa
Rahe sada parivar
Santan sukh vaibhav badhe
Mite paap sanhar
meaning:
|| Shri Ahoi Mata Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Ahoi Mata vinati suno
(O Ahoi Mother, please listen to my prayer.)
Sevak ki rakho laaj
(Protect the honor of your servant.)
Santan sukh samriddhi do
(Grant the happiness of children and prosperity.)
Puran ho sab kaaj
(May all my tasks be fulfilled.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Ahoi Ambe Jagadamba
(Victory to Ahoi, Mother of the universe.)
Sada sahay karo sukh kamba
(Always help me and shower happiness.)
Nari tav vrat karti pyari
(Women perform your lovely fast.)
Sut sukh paaye sada sukhkari
(They always receive the happiness of children, which is blissful.)
Nirdhan dhani heen sukh paave
(The poor gain wealth and the destitute gain happiness.)
Daridra mite vaibhav barsaave
(Poverty vanishes and you shower prosperity.)
Sinhasin tu jagat Bhavani
(O Bhavani of the world, you sit on the throne.)
Bhakt bachavan wali rani
(O Queen, you are the protector of devotees.)
Dhoop deep naivedya chadhava
(Those who offer incense, lamps, and offerings.)
Bhakt manovanchhit phal paava
(The devotees obtain the desired fruits.)
Putravati nari sukh paave
(Mothers of sons find happiness.)
Putraheen ke putra basaave
(You bless the sonless with children.)
Sukh sampatti dehi Jagadamba
(O Jagadamba, grant happiness and wealth.)
Dukh daridra mite sab kamba
(May all sorrows and poverty be destroyed.)
Ashtami tithi vrat jo nari
(The woman who performs the fast on Ashtami tithi.)
Karahi shraddha sahit taiyari
(Prepares for it with devotion.)
Sada suhagin vah nari hoi
(That woman remains eternally blessed with a husband.)
Kripa karen Jagjanani Ahoi
(May Jagjanani Ahoi shower her grace.)

|| Doha ||
Ahoi Mata ki kripa
(May the grace of Ahoi Mother.)
Rahe sada parivar
(Always remain upon the family.)
Santan sukh vaibhav badhe
(May the happiness of children and prosperity increase.)
Mite paap sanhar
(May all sins be destroyed.)

faq.q1: Why is the Ahoi Chalisa recited?
faq.a1: Ahoi Mata is considered the protector of children and the granter of long life. This Chalisa is recited for the good health, happiness, and bright future of one's children.
faq.q2: What is the best day to recite the Ahoi Chalisa?
faq.a2: It is best to recite it on Ahoi Ashtami, but mothers can recite it regularly or on any auspicious day for the safety and well-being of their children.
faq.q3: Can only mothers recite the Ahoi Chalisa?
faq.a3: It is usually recited by mothers for their children, but any family member can recite it while meditating on Ahoi Mata for the welfare and happiness of the children.
faq.q4: Does reciting it help in improving children's health?
faq.a4: Yes, it is a deeply held belief that reciting the Ahoi Chalisa with full devotion averts crises and helps maintain good health for children.
faq.q5: Is it necessary to observe a fast (Vrat) to read it?
faq.a5: Observing a fast is significant on Ahoi Ashtami, but you can recite the Chalisa on normal days without a fast, simply with a devotional heart.
